name: Cairo Governorate (Egypt)

colors.symbolism: the Nile and the sky over the capital
the Citadel of Saladin, mosques and other historic buildings of Islamic Cairo

description: A light blue field charged in the centre with a brown silhouette of the Citadel of Cairo and its mosques and minarets.
history: Cairo Governorate is coterminous with the city of Cairo. The flag — a light blue field defaced with a brown stylised silhouette of the Citadel of Saladin and the mosques of Islamic Cairo — was adopted as part of the system of governorate flags introduced in the 2000s.

features.symbolism: the Nile and the sky over Cairo

features.role: Citadel of Saladin and mosques of Cairo
features.symbolism: a stylised silhouette of the Cairo Citadel and its minarets — defining landmarks of the historic Islamic city
